title: Come and See
subtitle: The first disciples leave John, follow the Lamb, and ask where He dwells

stillness: Like studious disciples, do not be content to taste the truth in passing.
Seek a dwelling-place, that you may be more fully taught by Him.
gospel: Again John was standing, and two of his disciples with him.
And looking upon Jesus as He walked, he says: Behold the Lamb of God.
And the two disciples heard him speaking, and they followed Jesus.
And Jesus, turning and seeing them following Him, says to them: What seek you?
They said to Him: Rabbi (which means, Master), where do you dwell?
He says to them: Come, and see.
They came and saw where He dwelt, and they stayed with Him that day.
It was about the tenth hour.
behold: Behold John, standing unmoved beside the Jordan, on the height of perfection, bending nowhere from the straightness of the truth.
He had borne witness of Christ to the crowds; here he bears it to his disciples.
Wonder at the simple, humble, and easy calling of the disciples, made without any objection or questioning.
Behold the kind Lord Jesus, thirsting for their salvation and the salvation of all, turning to them out of His goodness.
He who always turns to those who turn to Him, and receives those who come.
Seeing them with eyes of mercy and tenderness following after Him, that He might give them confidence and courage.
He had nothing on earth save the one title of Pilate; and for us many titles are not enough!
O how blessed a day, and how blessed a night, they passed, hearing Him and seeing Him.
Whom many, longing to see and to hear, neither saw nor heard.
consider: He does not say, Whom seek you?, for of that Person they were already enlightened by John.
But He says, What seek you?, for He knew they sought to learn something of salvation.
He asked it not from ignorance, as one who would learn, but to make them more familiar by the question.
That the Lord turns to them, that He looks upon them, that He speaks with them, is the sign of His mercy and good will.
Here we are given to understand that to all who begin to follow Christ with a pure heart, He gives confidence and the hope of mercy.
Chrysostom says: from this we are taught that when we have begun to will rightly, then He gives us many occasions of salvation.
They ask of His little lodging, not of His house, which He did not have; for the Son of Man had nowhere to lay His head.
Unless you have followed Him by good work, you will never attain to the vision of His face, nor reach His dwelling.
Come, He says, by believing and working well; and see, by understanding.
Come invites to action; see invites to contemplation.
Let us also build in our heart a place where He may dwell, and make within ourselves a house to which He may come.
That He may speak with us, and teach us.
prayer: O good Jesus, Redeemer of the lost, Saviour of the redeemed, hope of the exiled.
Sweet consolation of the poor in spirit, strength of the labouring, refreshment of the weary, crown of those who triumph.
The one reward of the citizens above, and their true gladness.
Renowned offspring of the most high God, and fruit of the virginal womb.
Fountain of all graces, of whose fullness we all have received.
Let me be borne toward You as the end of all things, reaching out, and hoping, and loving.
O Jesus, my desire! to You may I be led, You may I follow.
For You alone suffice, You alone save.
You alone are good and sweet to those who seek You and love Your name. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: O quam beatam diem, et quam beatam noctem duxerunt, audientes et videntes eum: quem multi videre et audire volentes, non viderunt neque audierunt!
latinJewel.english: O how blessed a day, and how blessed a night, they passed, hearing and seeing Him: whom many, longing to see and to hear, neither saw nor heard!
